nodes.body: <<FILL role=setup words=210 beats='Kaia Osei boards the final supply shuttle to the outpost at the reactor hums to life; the launch bay's clock has already stopped; four ways to read this strange, half-empty train open before her'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=165 beats='Kaia Osei turns toward the night navigator and his worn data slate; the hull creaks and the viewport holds her reflection over the void; a first detail snags her attention'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=180 beats='the night navigator and his worn data slate offers Kaia Osei two ways to read it; one is direct and risks notice, the other is patient and lets the final supply shuttle to the outpost keep its secret a while'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=150 beats='the direct approach to the night navigator and his worn data slate yields a hard, specific fact that will not sit still in Kaia Osei's mind; it points deeper into the final supply shuttle to the outpost'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=175 beats='watching the night navigator and his worn data slate from the edges, Kaia Osei can either fold the observation into what she already knows, or let the late hour and the motion pull her under before she has understood it'>>

choices.label: Hold the thread and go on.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=160 beats='both readings of the night navigator and his worn data slate converge on the same small wrongness; Kaia Osei feels the journey close around it like a held breath'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=170 beats='carrying what the night navigator and his worn data slate has told her, Kaia Osei moves on through the swaying cars toward the question the whole shuttle seems built around'>>

<<FILL role=setback words=150 beats='the pull of the night navigator and his worn data slate closes over Kaia Osei before she can hold it; she surfaces later having missed the moment, safe but emptied of the thing she almost understood'>>

<<FILL role=pivot words=205 beats='the first bottleneck, why the final supply shuttle to the outpost never seems to arrive; every clock aboard has stopped and the outpost beacon stays exactly one hour off, no matter how long the night runs'>>

choices.label: Find the child who never sleeps.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=pivot words=205 beats='the second bottleneck, who the passengers really are; each traveller seems to be riding for a reason older than tonight, and none of them will say where they first got on'>>

<<FILL role=pivot words=205 beats='the third bottleneck, the choice at the asteroid corridor; the final supply shuttle to the outpost slows before the asteroid corridor and Kaia Osei must decide what she will carry through the dark, and what she will leave'>>

choices.label: Ask the navigator for the whole truth.
Answer the mourning woman's quiet request to carry a message on.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=pivot words=205 beats='the passengers reveal themselves as the first pale system light touches the peaks; Kaia Osei understands the final supply shuttle to the outpost is a passage and not a route, and that how the night ends is now hers to shape'>>

choices.label: Ride on and simply be present.
Turn to the crewmate who needs you.
Chase the last of the truth.
Try to get off before the asteroid corridor.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=185 beats='Kaia Osei gives herself to the plain act of drifting on, wide awake; the carriage narrows to this one long, lucid stretch of track before the asteroid corridor'>>

choices.label: Follow it toward the first pale system light.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=180 beats='in the plain act of drifting on, wide awake there is a smaller door and a larger one; one closes the night early, the other stays open toward the first pale system light'>>

choices.label: Stay open to the first pale system light.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=185 beats='having chosen the plain act of drifting on, wide awake, Kaia Osei comes level with the first pale system light coming up over the ridgeline where all the night's threads seem to gather'>>

choices.label: Meet the first pale system light.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=setback words=150 beats='the calm Kaia Osei finds is real but slips through her fingers before the first pale system light; she arrives unsettled, sure only that she let a moment pass'>>

<<FILL role=resolution words=185 beats='Kaia Osei gives herself to the crewmate at the corridor end who needs a hand; the carriage narrows to this one long, lucid stretch of track before the asteroid corridor'>>

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=175 beats='Kaia Osei reads half the endless manifest's secret and no more; it is enough to know the final supply shuttle to the outpost is kind, not enough to know why it chose her'>>

<<FILL role=resolution words=185 beats='Kaia Osei gives herself to the cold airlock door and the idea of stepping out; the carriage narrows to this one long, lucid stretch of track before the asteroid corridor'>>

choices.label: Follow it toward the first pale system light.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=180 beats='in the cold airlock door and the idea of stepping out there is a smaller door and a larger one; one closes the night early, the other stays open toward the first pale system light'>>

choices.label: Stay open to the first pale system light.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=185 beats='having chosen the cold airlock door and the idea of stepping out, Kaia Osei comes level with the first pale system light coming up over the ridgeline where all the night's threads seem to gather'>>

choices.label: Meet the first pale system light.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=setback words=150 beats='the door opens onto the first the launch bay of all, and Kaia Osei wakes there as the final supply shuttle to the outpost pulls away northward without her, the night unspent'>>

ending.title: Back at the First Launch Bay

nodes.body: <<FILL role=pivot words=200 beats='every route through the night gathers at the first pale system light bottleneck above the asteroid corridor; the peaks go gold, the passengers are quiet, and the last shape of Kaia Osei's journey waits on one final turning'>>

choices.label: Set the night down and stay.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=190 beats='Kaia Osei stops trying to arrive; she lets the outpost beacon stay one hour off forever and finds, at last, that she does not mind the distance'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=180 beats='the seat beside the mourning traveler is warm; Kaia Osei can stay through the asteroid corridor at the price of her own arrival, or step back and keep it'>>
<<FILL role=resolution words=190 beats='Kaia Osei speaks the outpost beacon's whole quiet truth aloud to the grey window, and the naming of it is its own kind of arrival'>>

<<FILL role=resolution words=185 beats='Kaia Osei asks the final supply shuttle to the outpost for nothing but a safe waking, and the final supply shuttle to the outpost, being kinder than she feared, grants exactly that and no more'>>

<<FILL role=success words=220 beats='the best ending: Kaia Osei rides the final supply shuttle to the outpost with open eyes and an open heart, at peace with a journey that was never meant to end at a station; the first pale system light holds and she is finally, wholly here'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=215 beats='Kaia Osei stays beside the mourning traveler through the asteroid corridor and delivers her safely into the first pale system light; helping another home turns out to be the arrival Kaia Osei was riding toward all along'>>

<<FILL role=setback words=150 beats='Kaia Osei stays past every reasonable hour and the first pale system light slips by; the traveler is helped but Kaia Osei surfaces frayed and far from herself, the good deed shadowed by what it cost'>>

<<FILL role=discovery words=185 beats='Kaia Osei names the outpost beacon for what it is, a passage the season lends to travelers who are ready; she does not stay, but she leaves knowing, and the knowing settles something in her'>>

<<FILL role=setback words=165 beats='Kaia Osei wakes at her own quiet station, unharmed and a little haunted; the final supply shuttle to the outpost is gone, the night unexplained, and she carries only the sense of a door she chose not to walk through'>>
